violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer and mixer attachment) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Utensils were moved to warewashing to be washed, rinsed, and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Calzones and pasta were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep and covered. Foods moved to shallow sheet pans to properly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026

The following was observed: 1) Knife in between prep until and prep table 2) Utensil in stagnant water 3) Ice scoop on unclean surface Utensils were relocated at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026

The cutting board in the of the prep unit is very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2026

The following was observed of non-food contact surfaces: 1) An accumulation of food debris was observed above the mixer attachment of the mixer 2) The guard of the ice machine was observed with an accumulation of black residue New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026

The floors in the basement and bar area has an accumulation of old rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026

The dishwasher failed to wash his/her hands in between loading soiled and unloading clean dishes from the dishwasher. Food employees must clean their hands after handling soiled equipment or utensils. New Violation. Correct By: 08/26/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Pasta soup was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Per staff, food was placed in the refrigerator under 2 hours prior to inspection. All soup moved to shallow pans and into the walk-in freezer to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/26/2024

The floor of the walk-in cooler was observed in poor repair (metal sheets tenting/loose). Equipment components such as doors, seals, hinges, fasteners, and kick plates shall be kept intact, tight, and adjusted in accordance with manufacturer's specifications. New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2024
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine in the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2024
